body:
Fixes #12

## Summary

This PR delivers both the **vision synthesis** for issue #12 and the first concrete **universal solver** implementation that turns that vision into running code. Telegram, the OpenAI-compatible REST surface, and the Rust library now share the same 11-step reasoning pipeline instead of dispatching to bespoke intent helpers.

### Documents
- Root `VISION.md`, `GOALS.md`, `NON-GOALS.md` for the link-native associative direction, extended with the universal solver model.
- `docs/case-studies/issue-12` (raw issue/PR evidence, external research, holistic requirements, root-cause analysis, solution plan, boundaries).
- `README.md` + `docs/REQUIREMENTS.md` updated through issue #12.

### Universal 11-step solver loop
Every reply now walks the same pipeline regardless of intent: **impulse → formalization → context → history → decomposition → TDD → synthesis → combination → verification → simplification → documentation**. The loop is implemented in `src/solver.rs` / `src/solver_helpers.rs` and wired into `FormalAiEngine.answer` so library, HTTP, and Telegram callers all share it.

### Append-only event log
`src/event_log.rs` records each step with content-addressed FNV-1a 64-bit ids. Steps surface in `evidence_links` and in a Links Notation `steps:` trace block; HTTP/Telegram replies advertise the trace pointer so callers can drill in.

### Knowledge graph projection
`GraphNode` / `GraphEdge` / `KnowledgeGraph` and a new `/v1/graph` endpoint serve the graph in Links Notation, JSON, or Graphviz DOT.

### Multilingual impulse tagging
Unicode-block based language detection (`Language::{English, Russian, Hindi, Chinese, Unknown}`) tags every impulse with `language:<slug>` evidence.

### OpenAI surface upgrades
- `rate_limit` metadata on `/v1/models`
- SSE streaming for `/v1/chat/completions` when `stream: true`
- `SolverConfig` (offline mode, autonomy bounds) driven by environment variables

### Telegram trace pointers
Every reply ends with a `/trace <id>` footer so users can request the inline event log for the message they just received.

## TDD MVP test suite
`tests/unit/mvp/` pins down every current and future MVP feature drawn from `VISION.md`, `GOALS.md`, `NON-GOALS.md`, and `docs/REQUIREMENTS.md` (R55–R71). Tests that the universal solver now satisfies have been un-ignored; remaining `#[ignore = "MVP-target: ..."]` tests still document the road ahead without blocking CI. Run all of them with `cargo test -- --include-ignored`.

| Module | Surface or invariant pinned down |
| --- | --- |
| `chat_surface` | bounded chat, identity, greeting, diagnostics defaults |
| `code_generation` | top programming languages, execution evidence, unsupported-execution honesty |
| `multilingual` | English / Russian / Hindi / Chinese baseline conversations |
| `openai_compatibility` | Chat Completions, Responses, HTTP routes, streaming, rate-limit metadata |
| `telegram_surface` | private / public chats, code formatting, message chunking, trace footer |
| `links_network` | doublet links, dynamic types, add-only history, concept uniqueness, trace links |
| `reasoning_loop` | impulse → local → external → decomposition → candidates → validation → smallest sufficient answer |
| `source_cache` | external source caching with provenance and TTL |
| `agent_isolation` | chat-vs-agent autonomy boundary and isolated execution |
| `translation_via_links` | Links Notation as the language of meaning |
| `network_visualization` | optional link-graph view alongside chat |
| `transparent_state` | querying the network through chat without leaking internal state by default |

## Verification
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --all-features --verbose`
- `cargo test --doc --verbose`
- `cargo test -- --include-ignored` (runs MVP-target expectations; remaining ignored tests document not-yet-implemented behavior)
- `rust-script scripts/check-file-size.rs`
- `rust-script scripts/check-changelog-fragment.rs`
- `git diff --check`
- `npm --prefix tests/e2e run test:local`

## Sources
External sources are cited in `docs/case-studies/issue-12/README.md`, including Deep.Foundation, Link Foundation tools, doublets-rs, Wikidata/Wikifunctions/Rosetta Code, WebVM/CheerpX, and link-assistant/agent.
title: Issue 12: vision synthesis + universal 11-step solver

body:
Fixes #14

## Summary

This PR delivers the **unified-surface** vision from issue #14: every interface — the GitHub Pages demo, Telegram bot, CLI, library, and HTTP server — now routes through the same universal 11-step solver pipeline. The demo's hardcoded prompt→answer table has been replaced with live solver handlers that mirror `src/solver.rs`.

### Unified demo worker (`docs/demo/formal_ai_worker.js`)
- Rewrites the worker from a lookup-table (`answers[code]`) to a `solve(prompt, history)` function that runs the same handler cascade as the Rust solver: greeting → identity → conversation memory → arithmetic → JavaScript execution → concept lookup → hello-world → unknown fallback.
- Every answer carries `intent`, `confidence`, and `evidence` arrays so the diagnostics panel works identically across surfaces.
- Hello-world programs for non-JavaScript languages now honestly report that the browser sandbox cannot invoke the toolchain instead of faking execution status.

### Arithmetic evaluator (`src/arithmetic.rs`, mirrored in worker)
- Precedence-climbing parser (primary → unary → multiplicative → additive) handles symbolic operators (`+`, `-`, `*`, `/`, `%`, `×`, `÷`), English-word operators (`plus`, `minus`, `times`, `divided by`, `modulo`), parentheses, decimals, negative numbers, and thousand separators.
- Division-by-zero and overflow produce clean error messages instead of panics.

### Offline concept knowledge base (`src/concepts.rs`, `data/seed/concepts.lino`)
- 9 seeded records: universal solver, event log, Links Notation, doublet, Wikipedia, Wikidata, Wiktionary, WebAssembly, Rust.
- `extract_concept_term` parses "what is X?", "tell me about X", "define X", "explain X", etc.
- `lookup_concept` matches by term, alias, or slug with case-insensitive normalization and article stripping.
- Every answer cites its source and source kind.

### JavaScript execution handler
- In the Rust solver (`try_javascript_execution`): honestly reports that the Rust runtime has no JS engine and advises running the snippet in a browser or Node.js.
- In the demo worker (`tryJavaScriptExecution`): actually executes the snippet in a sandboxed `new Function` call, captures `console.log` output, and reports errors.

### Multi-turn conversation memory (`ConversationTurn`, `ConversationRole`)
- `solve_with_history` threads prior turns into the event log as `prior_turn:user` / `prior_turn:assistant` events.
- Handlers: `try_recall_name` (scans history for "My name is X"), `try_recall_last_question`, `try_summarize_conversation`.
- The demo passes `history` from the React state to the worker on every message.

### localStorage persistence with Links Notation (`docs/demo/preferences.js`)
- `FormalAiPreferences.save()` encodes `{ demoMode, diagnosticsMode }` as a Links Notation doublet tree under the `formal-ai.preferences.v1` localStorage key.
- `FormalAiPreferences.load()` parses it back, with graceful fallback to defaults.
- `app.js` reads preferences on mount and writes on every toggle change.

### Module split refactor
- Extracted `src/arithmetic.rs` (335 lines): arithmetic evaluator types and functions.
- Extracted `src/concepts.rs` (220 lines): `ConceptRecord`, `CONCEPTS`, `extract_concept_term`, `lookup_concept`.
- Extracted `src/solver_handlers.rs` (626 lines): all `try_*` handler free functions and `finalize_simple`/`build_evidence_links`.
- `src/solver.rs` reduced from 1191 → 518 lines; `src/solver_helpers.rs` reduced from 1212 → 663 lines.
- All files now under the 1000-line cap enforced by `scripts/check-file-size.rs`.

### Testing
- `tests/unit/mvp/reasoning_paths.rs` (273 lines): arithmetic evaluation (operators, precedence, division-by-zero, English words), concept lookup (term, alias, unknown, citation), JavaScript execution detection, conversation history (name recall, previous question, summary).
- `examples/universal_solver_tour.rs`: end-to-end walkthrough of JS execution, source refresh, translation, and multi-turn conversation recall.
- `examples/try_arithmetic.rs` + `experiments/try_arithmetic.rs`: quick arithmetic evaluator smoke tests.

### Documentation
- `docs/REQUIREMENTS.md`: R83–R89 covering unified demo, localStorage persistence, arithmetic, concept lookup, conversation memory, JS execution, and case study.
- `docs/case-studies/issue-14/README.md`: full case study with raw data, online research, requirement analysis, solution plan, and verification evidence.
- `changelog.d/20260515_120000_issue_14_unified_demo.md`: changelog fragment for release.

## Verification

- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` ✓
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings` ✓
- `cargo test --all-targets` — 171 passing (31 lib + 2 integration + 138 unit), 78 ignored, 0 failed ✓
- `cargo test --doc` ✓
- `rust-script scripts/check-file-size.rs` — all files within limits ✓
- `cargo run --example universal_solver_tour` — JS handler, source refresh, translation, multi-turn recall all produce correct output ✓

## Requirements Coverage

| ID | Requirement | Where |
| --- | --- | --- |
| R83 | Route every demo answer through the universal solver — no hardcoded table | `docs/demo/formal_ai_worker.js` rewritten with `solve()` pipeline |
| R84 | Persist demo/diagnostics toggles in localStorage using Links Notation | `docs/demo/preferences.js` + `app.js` integration |
| R85 | Solve arithmetic prompts with symbols, English words, parens, decimals, modulo | `src/arithmetic.rs` + `try_arithmetic` handler + worker mirror |
| R86 | Answer "what is X?" from seeded Wikipedia/Wikidata/Wiktionary data with source citation | `src/concepts.rs` + `data/seed/concepts.lino` + `try_concept_lookup` |
| R87 | Remember conversation: recall name, previous question, summarize prior turns | `ConversationTurn`/`solve_with_history` + `try_conversation_memory` |
| R88 | Declare JS execution capability — Rust explains no runtime, browser actually executes | `try_javascript_execution` in solver + worker's `tryJavaScriptExecution` |
| R89 | Compile case-study evidence under `docs/case-studies/issue-14/` | `README.md` + `raw-data/` with JSON evidence |
title: Unify demo with universal solver; add arithmetic, concept, JS execution, and memory handlers

body:
## Issue #395

The deployed wasm assistant answered the Russian prompt
`У меня есть числа 3, 5, 6, 7, 8 отсортируй их в JavaScript, дай мне код и результат`
with `intent: unknown`. It must instead route to `write_program`, emit idiomatic
JavaScript that sorts the given numbers, and show the deterministically computed
result. The solution is meaning-driven (no hardcoded values), multilingual
(en/ru/hi/zh), universal (a whole family of numeric-list operations), and shared
by the Rust solver and the web worker.

## Code generation is discovered at execution time

Per the review request ("truly general, truly dynamic"), code generation is now
seed data, not code. `data/seed/coding-idioms.lino` declares, per language, a
scaffold for each operation family plus named idioms — code fragments whose
cases are selected by operation and value class — inherited through `extends`
(TypeScript extends JavaScript). The composer
(`src/solver_handlers/numeric_list/codegen.rs`, mirrored in
`src/web/formal_ai_worker.js`) discovers the composition during execution: it
walks the language's inheritance chain, picks the scaffold for the program's
operation family, and recursively expands `{idiom}` slots. There are no
per-language renderer functions left; covering a new language or coding task is
a seed-data change. Composition failures are explicit (`None`/`null`), never
silent fallbacks.

Cross-runtime equivalence is proven exhaustively:
`examples/numeric_list_matrix.rs` dumps the Rust engine's answers for all 170
(operation × language × value class) cells and
`experiments/issue-395-cross-runtime-codegen-parity.mjs` replays the identical
prompts through the worker, requiring byte-identical answers — 170/170 pass.

## CST/AST engine: link-foundation/meta-language

Generated programs are validated through
[link-foundation/meta-language](https://github.com/link-foundation/meta-language)
0.39 (`meta_language::LinkNetwork`) as the **sole, mutable CST/AST engine** — no
direct tree-sitter usage. `src/coding/cst.rs` parses each rendered program
through the links network and accepts it only when the parse produces real
`LinkType::Syntax` links, round-trips the source text, and reports no errors.

- meta-language 0.39 ships real tree-sitter grammars for **all 10 targets**:
  JavaScript, Python, Rust, Java, C#, C, C++, TypeScript, Go, Ruby — so every
  language validates through the same links network.
- TypeScript, Go and Ruby used a thin direct tree-sitter bridge in earlier
  revisions of this PR while meta-language gained coverage. Those gaps were
  **reported upstream and resolved**, and the bridge has since been removed:
  - TypeScript → [meta-language#41](https://github.com/link-foundation/meta-language/issues/41) (closed)
  - Go → [meta-language#42](https://github.com/link-foundation/meta-language/issues/42) (closed)
  - Ruby → [meta-language#43](https://github.com/link-foundation/meta-language/issues/43) (closed)
- The remaining missing upstream feature is **reported**:
  [meta-language#64](https://github.com/link-foundation/meta-language/issues/64)
  — render target-language source from a programmatically constructed syntax
  network (unparse). meta-language can parse and round-trip existing source but
  cannot yet emit code from a links network built via `insert_*`, so the
  composer renders text from the coding-idioms seed and meta-language validates
  it after the fact. With #64, generated code becomes valid by construction.

The engine for each language is declared in `data/seed/program-cst-grammars.lino`.
The trace records `synthesis:cst_engine` (`meta_language`) and the CST evidence
via `synthesis:cst_tree`:

```text
synthesis:cst_engine meta_language
synthesis:cst_tree cst_tree
  language javascript
  engine meta_language
  component meta-language
  source_repository https://github.com/link-foundation/meta-language
  language_label javascript
  projection concrete_syntax
  syntax_link_count 45
  has_error false
  text_preserved true
```

## How to reproduce / verify

- `cargo test issue_395 -- --nocapture` — the exact Russian prompt routes to
  `write_program`; an unsorted English JS prompt computes the sorted result; a
  quoted-string JS sort uses `value_type string`; the trace shows
  `component meta-language` and `has_error false`.
- `cargo test --test source cst -- --nocapture` — every catalog language has CST
  metadata and validates through meta-language with real `LinkType::Syntax`
  links; the formerly bridged languages (TypeScript/Go/Ruby) now parse through
  the same links network.
- `cargo test program_synthesis -- --nocapture` — Python synthesis logs
  `synthesis:cst_engine` / `synthesis:cst_tree`.
- `cargo run --example numeric_list_execution` — compiles and runs every
  generated program and asserts stdout equals the solver's computed result.
- `node experiments/issue-395-cross-runtime-codegen-parity.mjs` — byte-compares
  all 170 (operation × language × value class) answers between the Rust engine
  (`examples/numeric_list_matrix.rs`) and the worker mirror.
- `node --check src/web/formal_ai_worker.js` and
  `node experiments/issue-395-js-numeric-list.mjs` — web mirror.

## What changed

- `Cargo.toml`: `meta-language = "0.39"`; the direct `tree-sitter`,
  `tree-sitter-go`, `tree-sitter-ruby`, `tree-sitter-typescript` dependencies are
  gone.
- `src/coding/cst.rs`: every language routes through `LinkNetwork::parse`; the
  `CstEvidence::TreeSitterBridge` variant, the `tree_sitter_bridge` engine, and
  the bridge parser were removed.
- `data/seed/program-cst-grammars.lino`: declares `engine "meta_language"` for
  all ten languages.
- `data/seed/coding-idioms.lino`: the per-language coding knowledge base —
  scaffolds, idioms, names, types, mutability, inheritance — that both runtimes
  compose code from at execution time.
- `src/solver_handlers/numeric_list/`: universal, data-oriented list engine —
  `sort`, descending sort, `reverse`, `sum`, `product`, `minimum`, `maximum`
  over numeric lists, plus quoted-string list transforms; localized for
  en/ru/hi/zh; mirrored in `src/web/formal_ai_worker.js` (`tryNumericList`),
  including the seed-driven composer (embed regenerated with
  `experiments/generate-coding-idioms-embed.mjs`).
- Numeric-list and Python synthesis handlers log `synthesis:cst_engine`.
- CI: tests execute on ubuntu only (the suites themselves are kept) and
  superseded in-progress runs for older commits are cancelled
  (`concurrency` + `cancel-in-progress`).
- Docs (`docs/case-studies/issue-395/`), changelog fragment, and the
  `tests/source` mirror updated.

Full case study: `docs/case-studies/issue-395/README.md`.

Fixes #395
title: Validate generated programs with link-foundation/meta-language for issue #395

body:
## Summary

Closes #404. The prompt **"Забей мне 18 число в 17:00 по грузии на встречу с Леваном"** (and its English, Hindi, and Chinese equivalents) previously resolved to intent `unknown`. It now resolves to `calendar_create_event` and the assistant proposes a **real, importable calendar event** in every supported environment, "preferring the simplest available for the user at each situation" (per @konard's brainstorm note on the issue).

## What the solver now produces

The confirmation proposal carries two login-free, portable artifacts:

- **Universal RFC 5545 `.ics` VEVENT** — CRLF line endings, `DTSTART;TZID=`/`DTEND;TZID=`, escaped `SUMMARY`, and a stable content-derived `UID`. Imports cleanly into Apple Calendar, Outlook, Google Calendar, Thunderbird, and any other iCalendar client. This is the simplest method in the CLI and HTTP environments, where the user can save/import a file.
- **Google Calendar "render" template URL** — `calendar.google.com/calendar/render?action=TEMPLATE&text=…&dates=START/END&ctz=…`, which pre-fills a new event in the browser with no API token or server. This is the simplest method in a browser environment.

The core stays purely symbolic and deterministic: the solver *proposes* the event and invites confirmation rather than silently mutating a remote calendar.

## Multilingual support (en, ru, hi, zh)

Surface words — schedule verbs, "meeting"/"встреча"/"मीटिंग"/"会议", clock times, and timezone aliases such as "по грузии" → `Asia/Tbilisi` — live as self-describing meanings in `data/seed/meanings-calendar.lino`; the code knows only roles and English slugs. Hindi (verb-final) and Chinese (no word spaces) titles are trimmed of trailing/leading schedule-action fragments so the `.ics` SUMMARY keeps only the event and its participant.

## Reproduction

```
U: Забей мне 18 число в 17:00 по грузии на встречу с Леваном
A: (calendar_create_event) "Создать событие «Встречу с Леваном» на 18 число
   (2026-06-18). Время: 17:00, часовой пояс: Asia/Tbilisi. … BEGIN:VCALENDAR …
   https://calendar.google.com/calendar/render?action=TEMPLATE… Ответьте «да»…"
```

## Tests

- `tests/unit/specification/reasoning_paths.rs` — Russian, English, Hindi, and Chinese create-event tests assert the response carries a `BEGIN:VCALENDAR`/`BEGIN:VEVENT` document, the `Asia/Tbilisi` TZID, and a `calendar.google.com/calendar/render` URL.
- `tests/e2e/tests/multilingual.spec.js` — end-to-end calendar test across all four languages.
- Byte-for-byte Rust ↔ WASM parity verified: the `.ics` builder, Google Calendar URL builder, and title tidying are mirrored in `src/web/formal_ai_worker.js`.

Local gates all green: `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features`, file-size check, role-registry closure, multilingual intent coverage, full unit suite (849 passed), and the source mirror suite (397 passed).

## Notes

- Calendar export logic was extracted into `src/solver_handlers/calendar_ics.rs`, and the docs-method/how-to procedure reasoning-path tests were split into `tests/unit/specification/reasoning_paths_procedures.rs`, keeping every file under the repository's 1000-line limit.
- Version bump is driven by the changelog fragment `changelog.d/20260609_000000_issue_404_calendar_create.md` (`bump: minor`); `Cargo.toml` is left untouched per the release pipeline's version-check.

## Robustness: no false positives against installation prompts

The create-event recognizer originally treated any ASCII digit as a date signal. That let installation-conversion prompts (issue #423) which merely embed the word **"book"/"books"** plus an incidental digit — e.g. `EbookFoundation/free-programming-books`, `trimstray/the-book-of-secret-knowledge` — get hijacked into `calendar_create_event`. The gate now requires a genuine anchor: a **day-reference word OR a clock time** (localized lexicon surfaces plus a bare `HH:MM` scanner), and the schedule-verb fallback matches on **word boundaries** so "book" inside "books" never masquerades as the verb. The fix is mirrored byte-for-behavior across all three runtimes (Rust handler, the `tests/source` mirror, and the browser/WASM worker), and a dedicated parity experiment (`experiments/issue-404-js-calendar-create.mjs`) asserts the four multilingual create requests still route correctly while the installation prompts and bare digits are rejected.

`main` has been merged into this branch (now at `v0.193.0`); the PR is **mergeable** with no conflicts.
title: feat: natural language calendar event creation (#404)

body:
## Summary

Fixes #324 — a two-defect dialog bug, a new response-language setting, and a **running implementation** of the universal dynamic problem-solving vision (R4) for the program-modification case: requests are now reasoned into a Links Notation plan and lowered through the deterministic substitution engine using rules expressed as data.

### The reported dialog
1. **Bug 1 — wrong response language.** A Russian prompt ("Напиши мне программу на Rust, которая выдаёт список файлов в текущей директории") was answered in English. Language *detection* was already correct; the `write_program` answer prose was hard-coded English.
2. **Bug 2 — follow-up lost context.** "Сделай так, чтобы программа принимала путь как аргумент" routed to `write_program` but bound no task/language, surfacing "language `missing` and task `missing`".

## What changed

### R1 — Localized `write_program` answers
- **Rust** (`src/engine.rs`): intro, unsupported message, and execution report render per detected language for ru/hi/zh (English unchanged); Links Notation trace stays language-independent.
- **JS worker** (`src/web/formal_ai_worker.js`): `WRITE_PROGRAM_I18N` + `writeProgramStrings()` mirror the same localization so the GitHub Pages demo stays in parity.

### R2 — Response-language setting
- **App** (`src/web/app.js`): new `responseLanguage` (`last_message` default / `preferred` / `ui`) and `preferredLanguage` preferences — state, refs, persistence, reducer, and two `<select>` controls (Preferred language shown only in `preferred` mode).
- **i18n** (`src/web/i18n-catalog.lino`): `settings.responseLanguage` (+ `lastMessage`/`preferred`/`ui`) and `settings.preferredLanguage` for all four locales; catalog allow-list check updated.
- **Worker**: `responseLanguageFor(detected, preferences, userContext)` resolves the mode to a concrete language (with UI→browser-language→detected fallback) and `solve()` routes through it.

### R3 + R4 — Program modification via a Links Notation substitution pipeline
This is the core of the vision, now implemented as running, tested code rather than a hard-coded branch:

- **Reason** — `recover_write_program_rule` (Rust) / `recoverWriteProgramParameters` (JS) rebuild the missing task/language from the most recent prior turn and extract modifier slugs (`detected_program_modifiers`).
- **Plan in links** — `src/program_plan.rs` builds an inspectable Links Notation plan (`request:task -> <task>`, `request:modifier -> <modifier>`).
- **Lower via substitution rules** — the plan is rewritten to a fixpoint by the substitution engine (`src/substitution.rs`, issue #301) using rules defined entirely as **data** in `data/seed/program-plan-rules.lino`. The `path_argument_list_files` rule rewrites `request:task -> list_files` to `request:task -> list_files_arg` when a `path_argument` modifier is present. Adding a new `(modifier → task-variant)` rewrite (e.g. `count_only → count_files`) is pure rule data — proven by the `pipeline_is_data_driven` test in both Rust and the JS worker.
- **Compile** — the resolved task binds to the template catalog; the new **`list_files_arg`** task ships templates for all ten catalog languages and also works as an explicit single-turn request.
- **Trace (R6)** — the lowered plan is surfaced as a `write_program_plan:` evidence link and a `program_plan` Links Notation trace (base/resolved task, graph, applied rule sequence).

### R7 — Fix everywhere (parity)
Every behavioral change landed in **both** the Rust core and the JS worker. The worker carries a faithful mirror of the substitution engine and pipeline and reads the same canonical `data/seed/program-plan-rules.lino`; a `vm`-sandboxed parity experiment (`experiments/issue-324-js-worker.mjs`) asserts identical task resolution, the `write_program_plan` evidence link, the data-driven custom-rule case, and that the worker's parser produces the exact same ruleset as its embedded copy (no drift).

### R5 — Case study
`docs/case-studies/issue-324/` — timeline, all nine requirements, four root causes, per-requirement solution plans, existing-component review, and the universal-solver roadmap (Stages 2–3 now marked implemented, Stages 4–5 future work).

## How to reproduce / verify

```
cargo fmt --check                 # clean
RUSTFLAGS=-Dwarnings cargo clippy --all-targets   # clean
cargo test                        # 268 lib + 13 integration + 670 unit, all passed
node experiments/issue-324-js-worker.mjs          # all parity checks pass
node tests/e2e/scripts/check-i18n-catalog.mjs \
  && node tests/e2e/scripts/check-language-test-coverage.mjs \
  && node tests/e2e/scripts/check-language-change-parity.mjs \
  && node tests/e2e/scripts/check-multilingual-intent-coverage.mjs
```

New tests: `english_follow_up_modification_emits_substitution_plan_trace`, `pipeline_is_data_driven`, `russian_/hindi_/chinese_follow_up_path_argument_modification_reuses_context`, and `explicit_list_files_with_path_argument_is_supported` in `tests/unit/specification/code_generation.rs`, plus `src/program_plan.rs` unit tests.

### R8 — Upstream reports
None warranted: detection (`src/language.rs`) and the i18n runtime (`lino-i18n`) behaved correctly; the defects were entirely within this repo's engine/worker.
title: Fix issue #324: language-aware program writing, substitution-rule program modification, and response-language setting

## What This PR Does

Closes #244.

This PR carries the Issue #244 **vision-tracking and planning** work. Across six audit passes it progressively planned, re-scoped, and closed the issue batches needed to reach the deterministic, link-native universal problem solver the vision describes — and keeps every planning document in sync with the actual code state.

While this PR was in flight, the same final parity work it had planned (E33–E34) was **independently implemented and merged to `main`** via PRs #328 and #329. After re-merging `main` (now at **v0.149.0**), the net change of this PR relative to `main` is the **issue #244 case-study corpus plus the cross-document sync** that records the post-merge state. No Rust/JS source diff remains versus `main` — the parity code is already there; this PR makes the docs tell the truth about it.

### Vision-completeness conclusion (sixth-pass audit, 2026-05-29)

Every planned epic toward the vision is now closed and merged:

| Batch | Issues | Status |
| --- | --- | --- |
| E1-E14 (foundation) | #246-#259 | Closed / merged |
| E15-E20 (follow-ups) | #278-#283 | Closed / merged |
| E21-E27 (reasoning) | #298-#304 | Closed / merged (PRs #305-#311) |
| E28-E32 (synthesis) | #313-#317 | Closed / merged (PRs #319-#323) |
| E33-E34 (parity) | #326-#327 | Closed / merged (PRs #328-#329) |

**With E1-E34 all merged, no vision-planning epic remains open for issue #244.**

### What the parity batch (E33-E34) delivered (now on `main`)

- **E33 — universal multilingual operation vocabulary (#326, PR #328).** The text-manipulation handler no longer matches hardcoded English literals. Every operation is recognised by canonicalising the prompt against one shared seed table, `data/seed/operation-vocabulary.lino`, which lists each operation's surface forms in all four supported languages (`en|ru|hi|zh`). The Rust core loads it via `seed::operation_vocabulary()`; the browser worker loads the same file via `src/web/seed_loader.js`. Adding a surface form — or a whole language — is a **data edit, never a code change**.
- **E34 — cross-runtime parity (#327, PR #329).** The JavaScript browser worker (`src/web/formal_ai_worker.js`) routes synthesis prompts through `tryLinkNativeSynthesis`, `tryProgramSynthesis`, and `tryTextManipulation`, deriving the same synthesis/numeric/program/text answers as the Rust core. Parity is pinned by the shared fixture `data/parity/cross-runtime-synthesis.json`, the Rust test `shared_cross_runtime_synthesis_fixture_matches_rust_solver`, and the browser e2e `tests/e2e/tests/issue-327.spec.js`.

The synthesis step remains **general**: the universal 11-step loop derives answers by composing decomposed sub-results over the links network instead of resolving from seeded handlers, and the imported industry benchmark suite passes **10/10** with a `minimum_pass_count` ratchet — without per-case memorization.

### Docs synced in this PR

- **`ROADMAP.md`** — header sixth-pass note; the fifth-pass parity-gap narration reworded so it reads as subsequently closed; the parity batch table marks E33/E34 closed/merged.
- **`REQUIREMENTS.md`** — intro and the R250/R255 rows mark E33-E34 closed (PRs #328-#329); no vision-planning epic remains open. (Main's #327 section keeps R246-R249; the #244 planning section was renumbered to R250-R255 to avoid the ID collision.)
- **`ARCHITECTURE.md` §16/§17** — the parity batch rewritten as closed, with verified code anchors (`seed::operation_vocabulary()`, `src/web/seed_loader.js`, the three `try*` routing functions, the cross-runtime fixture and tests).
- **`VISION.md`** — parity gap marked closed; E33-E34 added to the merged-batches list.
- **`docs/case-studies/issue-244/`** — `README.md` and `proposed-issues.md` get timeline rows, status tables, and closed/merged headers for E33-E34, plus the full case-study corpus (raw data, online research, proposed issue bodies).

All referenced code anchors were verified present in the merged tree before committing.

### Verification

- Branch merged up to date with `main` (v0.149.0); merge status **CLEAN / MERGEABLE**.
- Post-merge: `cargo build`, `cargo test` (673 passed, 0 failed), `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy` all green.
- `tests/e2e/scripts/check-language-test-coverage.mjs` — passes (text-manipulation handler is byte-identical to `main`).
- No manual version edit (the `version-check` CI guard forbids it; releases are automated via changelog fragments on push to `main`). This PR is docs-only relative to `main`, so no changelog fragment is required.

## Summary

Prompts like **"Напиши скрипт на питоне"** (Russian: *Write a script in Python*) were returning `intent: unknown` because the symbolic engine only matched "hello world" code requests — not generic "write a script/program" phrasing.

- **Root cause**: `hello_world_program()` in `engine.rs` requires both the `hello` and `world` tokens, so any "write a script in Python" prompt fell through to `SelectedRule::Unknown`.
- **Fix**: Added a new `try_write_script` handler that detects "write a script/program/code in \<language>" phrasing in English, Russian (including inflected forms like *питоне*, *расте*), Hindi, and Chinese, and routes it to the matching program from the existing `HelloWorldProgram` catalog.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/engine.rs` | Add `hello_world_program_by_alias` — looks up a program by Latin alias or Russian colloquial name (питон→python, раст→rust, джаваскрипт→javascript, …) |
| `src/solver_helpers.rs` | Add `is_write_script_request` — detects write-script intents in EN/RU/HI/ZH; explicitly excludes "hello world" prompts so the existing rule keeps its intent |
| `src/solver_handlers.rs` | Add `try_write_script` handler — claims the impulse and returns the matching code block with execution report |
| `src/solver.rs` | Wire `try_write_script` into `handle_specialized_pattern` before `try_algorithm` |
| `tests/unit/formal_ai.rs` | Add `write_script_prompt_returns_code_block` regression test covering the original Russian prompt and EN/RU variants |

## How to reproduce the original issue

1. Open https://link-assistant.github.io/formal-ai/
2. Send: `Напиши скрипт на питоне`
3. **Before fix**: returns `intent: unknown` — "Я пока не знаю символьного правила для этого запроса."
4. **After fix**: returns `intent: write_script_python` with a Python code block

## Test plan

- [x] `cargo test write_script_prompt_returns_code_block` — new regression test passes
- [x] `cargo test hello_world_request_is_not_routed_to_greeting` — existing guard still passes
- [x] `cargo test` — all 176 tests pass, 0 failures
- [x] `cargo fmt --check` — no formatting issues
- [x] `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features` — no errors

Fixes #35

body:
## Summary

Closes [#185](https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/issues/185).

Replaces the unknown-fallback for proof-shaped prompts (`Prove …`, `Show that …`, `Докажи …`, `साबित कर …`, `证明 …`) with a real universal proof / disproof engine that **never refuses**, and makes the rendering **configuration-aware** so the two `SolverConfig` sliders directly steer the answer style.

### Configuration-aware rendering (addresses [comment 4510303127](https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/pull/199#issuecomment-4510303127))

| Slider | Default | Behavior at high values | Threshold |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `guess_probability` | `0.8` | Prepends a localized "How I interpreted the request" header that names the formal system; for `PartialPlan` outcomes inserts two extra steps — a closed-sentence translation `⟦φ⟧ = ∀x. P(x) → Q(x)` (Definition) and a `relative-meta-logic` verification step (Inference). | `≥ 0.6` |
| `follow_up_probability` (new) | `0.75` | Appends a localized "Clarifying questions" footer for `Disproven`, `PartialPlan`, and `Inconclusive` arms enumerating the axiom set, definition, and acceptance criterion the user still has to confirm. | `≥ 0.5` |

Both sliders are reachable from the web preferences UI, the Rust `SolverConfig` fields, and the environment (`FORMAL_AI_GUESS_PROBABILITY`, `FORMAL_AI_FOLLOW_UP_PROBABILITY`). Every render decision is auditable on the append-only log as `policy:proof_guess_probability`, `policy:proof_follow_up_probability`, `proof_render:interpretation`, and `proof_render:follow_ups`.

### Reproduction prompt from #185

Before:

```
A (intent: unknown): I cannot answer that from local Links Notation rules yet. …
```

After (default sliders):

```
A (intent: proof_request): How I interpreted the request: …

Proof plan (method: axiom-set reduction).

1. Hypothesis: Reduce "determinism" to a checkable arithmetical / dynamical
   statement: pick an axiom set A and the precise reading of determinism
   inside A.
2. Inference: Apply Picard–Lindelöf inside Newtonian mechanics …
3. Definition: Translate the claim into a closed sentence φ in the chosen
   axiom set (PA, ZFC or Newtonian mechanics). The translation matches
   the canonical relative-meta-logic encoding ⟦φ⟧ = ∀x. P(x) → Q(x) …
4. Inference: Hand ⟦φ⟧ to the relative-meta-logic library: it runs the
   selected tactic (rewrite / induction / contradiction) and returns
   either a signed proof certificate or a counterexample. …
5. Conclusion: …

Clarifying questions:
- Which axiom set should we work in (PA, ZFC, Newtonian mechanics, …)?
- How exactly should "determinism" be defined inside that axiom set?
- What is the acceptance criterion (Gödel-style limit ok or not)?
```

## What's in this PR

### `src/proof_engine/` (new module)

| File | Responsibility |
| --- | --- |
| `types.rs` | `ProofMethod`, `StepKind`, `ProofStep`, `Proof`, `ProofOutcome`, **`ProofRenderConfig`**. All carry multilingual labels. |
| `arithmetic.rs` | Recognises `<expr> ⟨op⟩ <expr>` (=, ≠, <, >, ≤, ≥), normalises Unicode operators, evaluates both sides with the exact arbitrary-precision calculator in `crate::arithmetic`, emits `Proven` or `Disproven`. |
| `library.rs` | Multilingual library of classical theorems: Pythagorean theorem (construction), Euclid's infinitude of primes (contradiction), √2 irrationality (contradiction), Fermat's little theorem (induction), Gödel's first incompleteness theorem (known-theorem citation), Laplacian determinism (axiom-set reduction). |
| `mod.rs` | `attempt_proof` and **`attempt_proof_with_config`**. Routes arithmetic → library lookup → Gödel + determinism combo → generic partial plan. Never refuses. High guess expands the plan with the deep-reasoning steps. |
| `presenter.rs` | `render_outcome` and **`render_outcome_with_config`**. Localizes headings (`Proof` / `Доказательство` / `प्रमाण` / `证明`), method labels, step labels, missing-inputs section, the new "Interpretation" header, and the new "Clarifying questions" footer. |

### Handler integration

`src/solver_handlers/user_intent.rs::try_proof_request_with_config` calls the engine, emits the `policy:*` / `proof_render:*` events, and hands the rendered body to `finalize_simple`. Confidence: `Proven`/`Disproven` → 0.85, `PartialPlan` → 0.6, `Inconclusive` → 0.4. `src/solver.rs::handle_specialized_pattern` builds a `ProofRenderConfig` from the live `SolverConfig` once per iteration and dispatches the proof-request handler through the config-aware entry point.

### Tests

* 13 baseline tests in `tests/unit/proof_request.rs` (intent routing, multilingual proofs, no-refusal contract).
* **16 new tests** in `tests/unit/proof_request_config.rs` covering the high-guess / low-follow-up axis, deep-reasoning step injection, multilingual interpretation/question labels, log-event emission, env overrides, and the `ProofRenderConfig` threshold helpers.
* Plus the existing in-module unit tests inside `src/proof_engine/*`.

Full suite: **604 passed, 0 failed, 69 ignored** (160 + 11 + 433).

### Docs

- `docs/case-studies/issue-185/README.md` extended with the configuration-aware rendering section, the new requirements (R185-14 .. R185-17), and the 16 new test descriptions.
- `changelog.d/20260520_200000_issue_185_proof_request.md` updated to describe both the universal prover and the new sliders.

## Test plan

- [x] `cargo fmt --all` — clean
- [x] `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings` — clean
- [x] `cargo test --all` — 604 passed, 0 failed, 69 ignored
- [x] `rust-script scripts/check-file-size.rs` — no new files near the 1000-line cap
- [ ] CI checks pass on remote
- [ ] Reproduction prompt verified end-to-end against the deployed surface (not possible locally; rely on the test suite)

Refs: #185

Fixes #185
title: Universal proof/disproof engine for proof requests (issue #185)

body:
## Summary

Fixes #340. A composite `write_program` request — *"Write a Rust program that: makes an HTTP GET request, parses the JSON response, calculates statistics (mean, median), outputs the results, with error handling and comments"* — used to dead-end on `write_program_unsupported` ("I do not have a template for language `rust` and task `missing`") because no single verified-catalog alias matched the multi-step task.

This PR adds a composite-program **blueprint** synthesizer that decomposes such a prompt into capabilities, matches a curated recipe, and returns a real, idiomatic multi-step program — together with a numbered decomposition plan, the required libraries, how-to-run instructions, and an **honest "not run"** execution report (the program needs external libraries and network access the offline sandbox cannot provide, so it never claims it "compiled and ran"). The emitted program is a *projection* of the decomposition along **two independent composition axes**, and the synthesis strategy is **switchable from settings**. Everything is applied to **both** source-of-truth engines (Rust core + JS worker) at byte-for-byte parity.

## Root cause

The defect is not in detection or language extraction — both are correct. The `write_program(language, task)` model resolves the **task** against a small set of *verified* aliases (`hello_world`, `count_to_three`, `list_files`, `list_files_arg`), each backed by a program the project actually compiled and ran. A composite prompt matches no single alias, so the formalizer returns `UnsupportedWriteProgram { task: None, language: Some("rust") }` and the engine renders the dead-end. The engine had no mechanism to decompose a prompt into capabilities and assemble a program from them — and such a program can never join the verified catalog because it cannot run offline. The missing piece is a **separate, honest path** for real-but-not-run programs.

Full analysis: [`docs/case-studies/issue-340/README.md`](https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/blob/issue-340-dbe3f3891f1b/docs/case-studies/issue-340/README.md).

## Compositional synthesis — two independent axes (not memoization)

The synthesized program is a **projection of the decomposed capabilities**, never a frozen string — the smallest honest demonstration that the program is *assembled* from the decomposition rather than recalled from a table (`NON-GOALS.md`). Two orthogonal axes drive the projection:

- **`comments` axis** — when the request asks for comments the documented program is emitted; otherwise every whole-line comment (and a leading Python module docstring) is stripped. Whole-line comment removal is provably non-semantic in every supported language, so the stripped program still compiles byte-for-byte.
- **`error_handling` axis** — optional defensive blocks are wrapped in `// region:error_handling … // endregion:error_handling` markers (`#` for Python/Ruby): the Rust empty-input guard, the Python `raise_for_status` + empty-list guard, and the JavaScript `!response.ok` + empty-array guard. The marker lines are **always** stripped from output; the region body is kept only when the request asks for error handling. Each region is whole-statement, so dropping it leaves a still-compilable program.

The axes are orthogonal, so one recipe yields the full **cross-product of four** distinct, still-compilable programs — `documented`, `comments_only`, `errors_only`, `stripped`.

## Switchable composition strategy (`BlueprintComposition`)

Per maintainer request ("try all directions, allow switching between them in settings, use the most promising by default"), the synthesis strategy is a first-class setting:

- **`Composed`** *(default — most promising)*: project the program from the decomposed capabilities (drop unrequested regions/comments as above).
- **`Documented`**: always emit the fully annotated program — every region body and every comment kept (only the internal directive lines are removed).

The setting is threaded end-to-end: `SolverConfig.blueprint_composition` (+ `FORMAL_AI_BLUEPRINT_COMPOSITION` / `FORMAL_AI_PROGRAM_COMPOSITION` env overrides), the solver handler, the JS worker, a demo-UI dropdown (toggleable by natural language — "documented programs", "полная документация", …), persisted in preferences, localized across all four lino-i18n locales (en/ru/hi/zh), and reported in the self-facts inventory as `relation "blueprint_composition"` across the Rust core, the JS worker, and the app.js local fallback.

![Program composition setting](https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/blob/issue-340-dbe3f3891f1b/docs/case-studies/issue-340/screenshots/program-composition-setting.png?raw=true)

## What changed

- **Blueprint synthesizer** (`src/coding/blueprint.rs` + extracted `src/coding/blueprint_tests.rs`): six multilingual capabilities (http_request, json_parse, statistics, output_results, error_handling, comments — matched in en/ru/hi/zh), one `http_json_stats` recipe with curated programs for Rust (`reqwest` + `serde_json`), Python (`requests` + `statistics`), and JavaScript (Node 18+ `fetch`), region-marker composition, and localized rendering.
- **Solver wiring** (`src/solver_handlers/program_blueprint.rs`, `src/solver.rs`, `src/lib.rs`): `try_program_blueprint` runs after issue #324 context recovery and before the unsupported dead-end, threading `BlueprintComposition`; returns `None` when no recipe matches so unmatched requests stay honestly unsupported.
- **Honest execution contract**: blueprints always report "not run — requires external libraries and network access" and never claim "compiled and ran"; the decomposition is recorded as `program_blueprint:` trace links + a `response:write_program:blueprint:<recipe>:<language>` evidence link, and the active strategy as `program_blueprint:composition:<mode>`.
- **Worker parity (R6/R7)**: `src/web/formal_ai_worker.js` mirrors the synthesizer byte-for-byte, including both composition axes and the switchable strategy.
- **Demo UI + i18n**: `src/web/app.js` setting (default + normalizer + NL command + persistence + prefs payload + dropdown `setting-blueprint-composition` + local self-facts); `src/web/i18n-catalog.lino` label + composed/documented options in en/ru/hi/zh.
- **File-size limits**: the `blueprint.rs` test module is split into `src/coding/blueprint_tests.rs` via the repo-idiomatic `#[cfg(test)] #[path = …] mod tests;` pattern; all Rust files stay under the 1000-line limit.
- **Case study + changelog fragment** (minor bump).

## How to reproduce / verify

- Before: the prompt in the issue returned `write_program_unsupported`.
- After: `cargo run --example repro_issue_340` renders the blueprint for English/Russian Rust, Python, Hindi, Chinese, and shows a partial request (no statistics) still staying honestly unsupported.
- Composition: `cargo run --example issue_340_emit_variants` writes the full four-variant cross-product (`documented`, `comments_only`, `errors_only`, `stripped`) per language to `target/issue-340-variants/`; every emitted Python variant passes `python3 -m py_compile` and every JavaScript variant passes `node --check` — i.e. each projection is a *different, still-valid* program.

## Tests

- Integration tests `tests/unit/specification/code_generation_blueprint.rs` (rust/python/javascript/russian blueprint + partial-stays-unsupported) and unit tests in `src/coding/blueprint_tests.rs`: region directives always stripped; comments kept when requested; comments stripped but logic preserved; `error_handling` axis composes independently of `comments`; stripped < documented; `Documented` strategy keeps every region and comment.
- Cross-engine parity: `node experiments/issue-340-worker-parity.mjs` (33+ checks: English/Russian Rust, Python, JavaScript; both `comments` and `error_handling` axes compose identically in both engines; `Documented` strategy; `normalizeBlueprintComposition` aliases; self-facts; partial and Go stay unsupported).
- `cargo test` (**704 passed** core + unit suites), `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `scripts/check-file-size.rs`, and the e2e guards (`check-i18n-catalog`, `check-language-test-coverage`, `check-language-change-parity`, `check-multilingual-intent-coverage`) all pass.
title: feat(#340): compositional write_program blueprints — comments + error_handling axes, switchable composition setting (both engines)

## Summary

Adds a themed, internationalized **`/download` page** for *formal-ai Desktop* — modelled feature-for-feature on [konard/vk-bot-desktop](https://github.com/konard/vk-bot-desktop) — together with the cross-platform desktop release pipeline that feeds it, the in-process-agent-by-default desktop shell with an opt-in local OpenAI-compatible server, and a full case study under `docs/case-studies/issue-347/`.

It also ships the full remainder of the requirement set in this same PR — **nothing is deferred**: local database sync (R5c), permission-gated local-execution routing + Docker sandbox (R5d), the Links-Notation REST layer + universal LinksQL (R6), and a first-party Anthropic→OpenAI adapter so `claude` targets the local server with no third-party proxy (R4).

Fixes #347.

Everything lives in this single PR (R10). The complete local CI gauntlet is green: `cargo fmt`, `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features` (0 warnings), file-size, `bun run build:web` + bundle-currency, all four i18n checks, the changelog-fragment and version-modification guards, the issue-103 text-policy meta-test, the full Rust suite (**714 + 322 + 13 passed, 0 failed**), the desktop `node --test` suite (**14 passed**) + smoke, and the Playwright local e2e suite (**235 passed**).

## How to reproduce / view it

```bash
# Serve the web app (which now includes /download) locally
bun run build:web && ./scripts/sync-seed.sh
npx serve src/web --listen 3456
# open http://localhost:3456/download/
```

The page auto-detects the visitor's OS, lists every cross-platform installer, fetches the latest release from the GitHub Releases API, verifies a downloaded artifact's SHA-256 **in the browser** against `SHA256SUMS.txt`, and honours the existing theme + locale switches (en / ru / zh / hi).

## Requirement-by-requirement mapping

| # | Requirement | Status | Where |
|---|---|---|---|
| **R1** | Linux / Windows / macOS builds | ✅ | `.github/workflows/desktop-release.yml` builds all three via electron-builder with explicit `artifactName` templates, `SHA256SUMS.txt`, `BUILD-PROVENANCE.txt` + SLSA attestation, and release-asset upload. |
| **R2** | `/download` page with *all* vk-bot-desktop features, themed, switchable, CI screenshots | ✅ | `src/web/download/{index.html,download.css,download.js}` — OS auto-detection + tabs, GitHub Releases grid, in-browser SHA-256 verification, build-provenance + macOS Gatekeeper guidance, theme + locale switching, CSP. CI-generated theme/locale screenshots under `docs/screenshots/issue-347/`. Feature parity tracked in the case study. |
| **R3** | In-process agent by default | ✅ | `desktop/main.cjs` runs the in-process reasoning agent by default; the page copy states this. |
| **R4** | Opt-in server API + docs for claude/codex/agent CLIs | ✅ | Local OpenAI-compatible server starts **only** when `FORMAL_AI_DESKTOP_SERVER` is set. `codex`/`agent` use `POST /v1/chat/completions` natively; `claude` is served by a **first-party** Anthropic→OpenAI adapter `POST /v1/messages` (`src/anthropic.rs`, full SSE streaming) — no third-party proxy. `docs/desktop/server-api.md` documents all three (bearer-token auth). |
| **R5a** | Reuse web-app code inside Electron | ✅ | The desktop shell loads the same `src/web` app (mirrored to `dist-web/` at build time via `desktop/scripts/prepare-resources.mjs`). |
| **R5b** | Web app auto-detects the local API server when on | ✅ | `src/web/app.js` routes chat to the local server only when the Electron bridge reports it ready; otherwise it stays in-process. |
| **R5c** | Local DB sync | ✅ | `src/memory_sync.rs` (`SyncStore` + union-by-id merge) behind `GET /v1/memory`, `GET /v1/memory/since`, `POST /v1/memory/import`; `desktop/lib/memory-sync.cjs` reconciles browser IndexedDB with the native store after each turn while server mode is on. `ROADMAP.md` §D1. |
| **R5d** | Route HTTP / tool calls / code execution to local app + dockers | ✅ | `desktop/lib/tool-router.cjs` — a **default-deny**, permission-gated dispatcher. `http_fetch`/`url_navigate`/`read_local_file` are served by the local process (reads confined to an allowed root); `eval_js`/`code_exec`/`shell` run **only** inside the `konard/box-dind:2.1.1` Docker sandbox with logs captured. Denied calls return a structured refusal and nothing executes; Docker absence refuses rather than running unsandboxed. `ROADMAP.md` §D2. |
| **R6** | lino-rest-api + universal LinksQL (extending link-cli) | ✅ | Links-Notation REST envelopes `GET /v1/bundle`, `GET /v1/links` (`knowledge_graph`), `POST /v1/links/query` (`links_query_result`), backed by the read-only LinksQL evaluator in `src/links_query.rs` (`MATCH (a)-[r]->(b) WHERE … RETURN …`). `ROADMAP.md` §D3. |
| **R7** | Only OpenAI-compatible REST; Links Notation preferred elsewhere | ✅ | The external REST surface stays OpenAI-compatible; the new `/v1/links*`, `/v1/bundle`, and `/v1/memory*` routes carry Links Notation payloads over REST, and internal seed/preference/i18n data stay in Links Notation (`.lino`). |
| **R8** | Apply CI/CD-template best practices; report shared issues upstream | ✅ | All four pipeline templates (js / rust / python / csharp) were fetched and compared file-by-file in the case study (`README.md` + `raw-data/*-template-tree.txt`). Writing the LinksQL grammar surfaced (and fixed) a real in-repo parser defect; no external library defect was found, so no upstream filing was required. |
| **R9** | Compile data to `docs/case-studies/issue-{id}`, deep study, requirements, solutions, existing components | ✅ | `docs/case-studies/issue-347/{README.md,ROADMAP.md,raw-data/}` — requirement breakdown, prior-art survey (agent, link-cli, lino-rest-api, lino-objects-codec), template comparison, solution plans. |
| **R10** | Plan + execute in this single PR | ✅ | This PR. |

## Tests that verify the change

- **`tests/e2e/tests/issue-347.spec.js`** — exercises the live page: every installer link resolves (R1), theme preference + live switching (R2), the locale switch localizes the *“Latest release”* heading and `<html lang>` and persists the choice across **all** supported languages (en / ru / zh / hi, asserting the native headings `Последний релиз` / `最新版本` / `नवीनतम रिलीज़`), offline SHA-256 verification of both matching and mismatched checksums, and CI screenshot generation per theme × locale.
- **`tests/unit/specification/desktop_surface.rs`** — pins the in-process-by-default / opt-in-server contract (R3/R4).
- **`tests/unit/local_surface.rs`** (10 tests) — drives the new server routes end-to-end: the Anthropic `/v1/messages` envelope + SSE stream (R4), the memory delta round-trip through `SyncStore` (R5c), and a role-filtered LinksQL result matching `/v1/graph` (R6).
- **`src/memory_sync.rs`**, **`src/links_query.rs`**, **`src/anthropic.rs`** carry inline unit tests for the merge, the LinksQL grammar (incl. the operator-scanner regression), and the Anthropic block flattening + SSE sequence.
- **`desktop/scripts/tool-router.test.mjs`** + **`desktop/scripts/memory-sync.test.mjs`** (14 `node --test` cases) — assert default-deny refusal, local-process `http_fetch`, `code_exec` inside `konard/box-dind` with logs, graceful refusal when Docker is absent, `read_local_file` root confinement (including sibling-prefix rejection), and the memory-sync watermark/delta logic. Run by `npm --prefix desktop test`.
- The change is gated by the repo's `check:language-test-coverage`, `check:language-parity`, `check:i18n`, and `check:intent-coverage` checks — all green.

## Screenshots (CI-generated, R2)

The page across themes and locales (committed under `docs/screenshots/issue-347/`, regenerated by the Playwright screenshot suite):

| | Light | Dark |
|---|---|---|
| **English** | ![en light](https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/blob/issue-347-4cd6329090e8/docs/screenshots/issue-347/download-en-light.png?raw=true) | ![en dark](https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/blob/issue-347-4cd6329090e8/docs/screenshots/issue-347/download-en-dark.png?raw=true) |

*(There is no “before” — `/download` is a brand-new page; these are the “after”.)*

## Implementation roadmap (all shipped)

The four largest, multi-subsystem efforts — local DB sync (D1/R5c), local-execution routing + sandbox (D2/R5d), the Links-Notation REST layer + LinksQL (D3/R6), and the bundled `claude` adapter (D4/R4) — each have their own design, code, and test rig, all landed here. `docs/case-studies/issue-347/ROADMAP.md` documents each with delivered-code references and acceptance criteria.
